paid_impact_focus: fund extraction, protocol value drain, reward extraction, and unfair reward access
gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?
attacker_profile: unprivileged user, depositor, withdrawer, claimant, borrower, liquidator, bridge user, router user, bot-discovered target user, or receiver contract
priority_surfaces: bridges and cross-chain claim or release paths
claim contracts, airdrop distributors, and vesting contracts
reward managers, fee splitters, and emission controllers
funded unverified contracts with public selectors
proxy contracts with live funds or recent implementation changes
bot-discovered target protocols repeatedly touched before profit sweeps
exploit_families: unauthorized withdrawal, redeem, unstake, claim, liquidate, release, sweep, or migrate
rounding that favors the attacker over repeated calls
phantom accounting where shares, debt, or rewards are created without matching assets
missing access control on value-moving functions
allowance, permit, or approval bugs that move user funds
bridge message validation gaps that unlock escrowed value
reward index desynchronization or stale accumulator state
zero-stake, dust-stake, flashloan, or front-run reward capture
proxy, implementation, or storage mismatch that exposes live funds
reject_classes: DoS, griefing, liveness, temporary freeze, UX, gas-only, or style-only issues
admin-only, governance-only, leaked-key, or malicious owner assumptions
pure third-party dependency failure without a protocol-controlled value path
theoretical math issues without live funds, rewards, or reproducible value movement
market competition, expected arbitrage, or normal public reward competition
known duplicate or stale context from another protocol
